title: Runtime IP address with static IPAM  CNI fails
body:
### Issue Description

Given that cni config
```
{
   "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
   "name": "testnet",
   "plugins": [
      {
         "type": "bridge",
         "bridge": "testnet",
         "isGateway": false,
         "ipMasq": false,
         "hairpinMode": false,
         "ipam": {
	"type": "static",
		"addresses": [
			{
				"address": "10.10.10.1/24",
				"gateway": "10.10.10.254"
			},
			{
				"address": "3ffe:ffff:0:01ff::1/64",
				"gateway": "3ffe:ffff:0::1"
			}
		],
		"routes": [
			{ "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }
		],
		"dns": {
			"nameservers" : ["8.8.8.8"],
			"domain": "example.com",
			"search": [ "example.com" ]
		}

 }
      }   
  ]
}
```

Running a container with specific IP fails

```
sudo podman run --rm --net=testnet:ip=10.10.10.100 -it  quay.io/karampok/snife /bin/bash
WARN[0000] Failed to load cached network config: network testnet not found in CNI cache, falling back to loading network testnet from disk
Error: plugin type="bridge" failed (add): cni plugin bridge failed: the 'ip' field is expected to be in CIDR notation, got: '10.10.10.100'

podman run --rm --net=testnet:ip=10.10.10.100/24 -it  quay.io/karampok/snife /bin/bash
Error: invalid option for network testnet: invalid ip address "10.10.10.100/24"
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  have a network with static cni IPAM
2. run a container and ask for specific IP address
3.


### Describe the results you received

Podman give error (?)

### Describe the results you expected

To work and see the IP address

comments.body:
https://www.cni.dev/plugins/current/ipam/static/

```
addresses (array, optional): an array of ip address objects:
address (string, required): CIDR notation IP address.

```

Our code is designed to work with the `host-local` local plugin.
Why are you trying to set a different ip from the one in the config? If you just do not set the ip on the podman cli the config should just use the ip from the file. I recommend you just switch to the host-local plugin if you wan this to work.
Closing as I think this works as expected right now.
I think the conclusion is that podman supports only host-local and does not support the static IPAM CNI (which is okay as that part is not standardized, can either podman or static IPAM be modified)
